race.name: Ylläs-Levi Hiihto
race.tagline: Seventy kilometers across three Lapland fells through a national park above the Arctic Circle — Finland's wildest marathon where the snow never lies.

vitals.registration: Online registration; typically open through March
vitals.aid_stations: 4 feed stations with warm drinks, energy bars, and Finnish pulla bread

climate.description: April in Finnish Lapland means spring snow conditions above the Arctic Circle. Temperatures range from -10°C to +5°C. Longer daylight hours but the fell crossings are fully exposed. Snow is virtually guaranteed at this latitude in April. Above-treeline sections face Arctic wind.
challenges: Arctic winds on exposed fell crossings
Spring sun causes rapid snow transformation during the race
Above-treeline sections with zero wind shelter
Remote wilderness course limits rescue access

course.primary: A point-to-point epic through Pallas-Yllästunturi National Park, crossing three major fells between Ylläsjärvi and Levi. The course passes through pristine Arctic wilderness, above and below the treeline, with significant climbing on each fell crossing. One of Finland's most challenging marathon courses.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks through national park terrain with fell crossings
course.grooming: Well-maintained Finnish national park trails, exposed sections can deteriorate in wind
features: Crosses three major fells in Pallas-Yllästunturi National Park
Above-treeline skiing with panoramic Arctic views
Pristine wilderness — one of Europe's last true frontiers
Finish at Levi ski resort with full facilities
April date means spectacular spring light conditions

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum scores for scenery and snow reliability reflect the Arctic wilderness setting and guaranteed spring snow. The fell crossings earn high technicality. Remote Lapland location limits accessibility and field size.

history.summary: Founded in 1985, the Ylläs-Levi Hiihto is one of Finnish Lapland's premier ski marathons. The 70km course through Pallas-Yllästunturi National Park represents some of the most challenging and scenic terrain in European marathon skiing. As a Euroloppet member event, it draws Nordic skiers seeking an authentic Arctic wilderness experience above the Arctic Circle.
notable_facts: Course passes through Pallas-Yllästunturi National Park, Finland's third-largest
Crosses three major fells — among the most vertical Euroloppet courses
Entirely above the Arctic Circle at 67°N latitude
April race date guarantees snow while offering long spring daylight
Finish at Levi, one of Finland's largest ski resorts

videos.title: Yllas - Levi 70, Runner vs Ski Race, round 2
videos.description:
My second attempt at a cross-country ski race, after about 300 km experience on the ski in my entire life. 
Yllas-Levi ski race classic style, 2018

To support this channel, you may do this on the price of a cup of coffee here: https://www.patreon.com/martinstefanov

Thanks a ton!
Martin

quotes.text: In the beginning it looks horrible, first 20 kilometers I think are the hardest but just don't give up just keep pushing and then after the 20km it's all downhill. The hardest part is behind and it's all relief from now on.
In this weather condition I have zero grip, zero. I think probably the ski is wet I don't know but I don't have any grip.
